narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away because a model sees a 76% chance you'll struggle with the bar exam feels deeply unfair, but let me walk you through the story it told about your path, based on the details you shared. Picture your journey starting strong in Year 1, landing you in the top decile of your school, but by Year 3, slipping to the 2nd decile—that downward trend in grades was the biggest signal to the model, whispering that sustaining the grind of law school might prove even tougher. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 20, which stood out as a clear hurdle, suggesting the intense analytical demands of bar prep could overwhelm without that sharper edge. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.1 added to the caution, as did being in the lower-middle family income bracket, which the model saw as limiting access to the extra tutoring or support that often tips the scales. As an Asian male going part-time, those factors together painted a picture of someone juggling real-world pressures that historically make bar passage a steeper climb. It wasn't one thing, but this combination—like a puzzle where each piece nudged toward tougher odds—that led to the tough call. The model isn't judging your potential or heart; it's just reading patterns from thousands like you. You've got strengths, like that early decile 1 grit, that could shine with targeted boosts—maybe LSAT retakes or grade recovery strategies. This isn't the end; it's a nudge to refine your story before the next door. Hang in there—you're capable of rewriting this narrative. What small step feels right for you now?
